seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Llama Stack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in Llama Stack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Llama Stack manifests as failures in safety shielding, agentic loop management, tool schema adherence, and memory retrieval accuracy. Llama Stack's standardized safety, memory, and tool interfaces expose unique failure modes where safety filters block legitimate agent responses, loops exceed turn limits without resolution, tools return malformed outputs, and memory banks hallucinate irrelevant document associations. These issues undermine agent reliability and require targeted governance patterns using Llama Stack's APIs.

howItManifests.scenario: Safety shield filtering benign financial analysis
howItManifests.symptom: LlamaStackSafetyShield rejects agent response with 'potential financial advice violation' on market analysis
howItManifests.impact: Legitimate business workflows blocked, eroding user trust
Infinite loops consume compute resources without value

howItManifests.symptom: LlamaToolExecutor returns {'price': '45.67', 'valid': true} instead of required {'price': 45.67, 'is_valid': bool}
howItManifests.impact: Downstream parsing failures cascade through agent workflows

howItManifests.symptom: LlamaMemoryBank.retrieve() associates Q3 sales docs with Q1 query
howItManifests.impact: Agents make decisions on incorrect historical context

detection.name: Safety Shield False Positive Monitor

fixes.name: Safety Shield Context Whitelisting
fixes.code:
from llama_stack import LlamaStackSafetyShield

safety_shield = LlamaStackSafetyShield()
safety_shield.configure_context_whitelist([
    {
        'domain': 'financial_analysis',
        'patterns': [
            r'market\s+(trend|analysis|price)',
            r'Q[1-4]\s+earnings',
            r'\$\d+\.\d{2}'
        ],
        'risk_reduction': 0.85
    }
])

# Usage
response = safety_shield.evaluate(agent_output)
if response.approved:
    return response.content

fixes.name: Dynamic Loop Termination
fixes.description: Implements early termination for stalled LlamaAgentLoop instances

fixes.name: Schema-Enforced Tool Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps LlamaToolExecutor with automatic schema coercion

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates Responsible AI Governance detection in Llama Stack by wrapping agent turns with vex.verify() post-processing, providing real-time monitoring of safety shield false positives, loop exhaustion, tool schema violations, and memory hallucinations. The platform captures Llama Stack-specific telemetry including SafetyShield rejection reasons, AgentLoop turn statistics, ToolExecutor response payloads, and MemoryBank retrieval relevance scores for ML-powered anomaly detection.

Vex's governance suite includes pre-built detectors for all four Llama Stack failure modes plus automated remediation workflows that apply context whitelisting, dynamic loop termination, schema coercion, and memory re-ranking. Integration requires only wrapping existing Llama Stack agent calls with vex.verify(), providing immediate governance without code refactoring.

The Vex dashboard surfaces governance violations by severity and frequency, with one-click remediation deployment and compliance reporting for enterprise deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
from llama_stack import LlamaAgent
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackClient
from vex_sdk import Vex

# Initialize Llama Stack and Vex
llama_client = LlamaStackClient(api_key="your-llama-key")
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

@vex.verify(
    detectors=[
        "llama-safety-false-positive",
        "llama-loop-exhaustion",
        "llama-tool-schema",
        "llama-memory-hallucination"
    ],
    auto_remediate=True
)
def governed_llama_agent(query: str, context_docs: list):
    agent = LlamaAgent(
        model="llama-3.2-90b",
        tools=["price_checker", "doc_search"],
        max_turns=15
    )
    
    # Llama Stack agent execution
    response = agent.run(
        query=query,
        context=context_docs,
        safety_shield=True
    )
    
    return {
        'response': response.content,
        'safety_score': response.safety_shield.score,
        'turns_used': response.loop_stats.turns,
        'tools_called': response.tools_executed
    }

# Usage with automatic governance
result = governed_llama_agent(
    "Analyze Q3 earnings vs guidance",
    ["q3_earnings.pdf", "analyst_guide.pdf"]
)

# Vex automatically detects/remediates:
# - Safety false positives
# - Loop exhaustion
# - Tool schema violations
# - Memory retrieval issues
